old_claim.note: Telecommunications provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The operational 1983 Internet interconnected packet-switched computer networks through gateways, making packet switching part of the scoped network architecture.

source_shape.source_locator: Sections 1.1-1.4 (pp. 1-2) define IP for interconnected systems of packet-switched computer communication networks and place internet modules in hosts and gateways.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: RFC 791 specifies an internetwork architecture that routes datagrams through packet-switched networks joined by gateways.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Packet switching names the directly specified networking mechanism, while telecommunications is broad background already inherited through the packet-switching and computer-networking lineage.

ontology_before: The Internet node used generic telecommunications as a direct enabler without naming the packet-network architecture of the 1983 internetwork.
ontology_after: The Internet node directly requires packet switching and retains generic telecommunications only through the existing networking ancestry.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-internet-operational-boundary: packet_switching is required and telecommunications is not a direct Internet prerequisite.
would_reject_if: A direct 1983 architecture source defines the scoped Internet without packet-switched networks or shows generic telecommunications to be the more specific causal boundary.
short_reason: RFC 791 defines the Internet architecture over interconnected packet-switched networks, replacing a generic telecommunications edge with the specified mechanism.
